question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 22.00% on 2026-04-07 for the Metaculus question "Will restrictions on the use of the Traditional Latin Mass in the Catholic Church be loosened during 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus community prediction for TLM loosening will be above 22.00% on April 7. The CP is currently exactly at the 22.00% threshold boundary, having dropped 3pp from 25% the prior week despite positive news (Pope Leo XIV's March 25 letter to French bishops calling for "generous inclusion" of TLM faithful). The historical CP range is 22-25% with mean 23.5%, meaning 22% is the observed minimum. Mean-reversion models favor a bounce back above 22% (~69%), while random walk models are near coin-flip (~45%). The key bearish signal is that the CP dropped despite the most positive papal rhetoric yet on TLM inclusion, suggesting forecasters are skeptical formal policy change will happen in 2026. The Vatican has explicitly stated Traditionis Custodes won't be overturned. With the CP right at the threshold and 9 days of possible movement, I estimate a slight edge toward YES due to mean-reversion from the historical minimum, tempered by the informative recent drop.

factors.description: Mean reversion: CP at 22% is the observed minimum over 5+ weeks of data, with historical mean of 23.5%. 4 of 5 prior weekly observations were above 22%.

CP dropped 3pp (25→22%) DESPITE positive news (Pope Leo's March 25 letter calling for 'generous inclusion' of TLM faithful). Forecasters appear to be discounting rhetoric as unlikely to produce formal change.
No formal loosening of restrictions has occurred. Vatican explicitly stated it will not overturn Traditionis Custodes. Only offering 2-year dispensations (pre-existing policy).
698 forecasters on underlying question - large stable base. Small movements are possible but large swings are unlikely over 9 days without a major catalyst.
Upcoming events: Pope's Algeria visit (April 13-15) and Holy Week/Easter could generate Catholic news coverage, potentially affecting forecaster attention and updates.
Threshold is extremely tight - CP needs to be strictly above 22.00%. Binary resolution at the exact current level creates near-50% base rate.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 55% probability (YES - CP will be above 22.00%)**

Key reasoning:
- The CP is currently at exactly 22.00% — right at the threshold boundary
- **Mean-reversion favors YES**: 22% is the observed minimum; the historical mean is 23.5%, and 4 of 5 prior weekly observations were above 22%
- **Bearish signal**: The CP dropped 3pp despite Pope Leo's strongest pro-TLM rhetoric yet (March 25 letter to French bishops), suggesting forecasters are skeptical formal policy change will happen in 2026
- **No formal action**: The Vatican explicitly won't overturn Traditionis Custodes, only continuing pre-existing dispensation policies
- With 698 forecasters and 9 days until resolution, moderate movement is plausible but large swings are unlikely without a major catalyst
- The slight edge to YES reflects the statistical tendency to bounce back from the observed minimum, tempered by the informative negative signal from the recent drop

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41174) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-07 18:13:48 is higher than 22.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 22.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-07 18:13:48, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
